items.title: Balcony composting without the smell
items.excerpt: Even in an apartment, a well-managed compost bin can reduce food waste without bothering the neighbors.
items.bodyMarkdown:
Balcony composting works best when the system stays simple enough to repeat every day. The goal is not to do a lot right away, but to keep the balance easy to maintain.

## Start with a reliable setup
Choose a compact bin with a lid that is easy to move and easy to clean. What matters most is that it fits naturally into your routine.

## Keep these materials nearby
- torn brown cardboard
- a small container for scraps
- a light scoop or spoon for mixing

## Prevent odors
After adding wet scraps, cover them with a small layer of dry material. That one gesture often makes the whole setup much easier to live with.

## Build a lasting habit
Start small for two weeks, notice what rhythm feels realistic, and only then increase the volume.

items.title: Build a simple recycling station in your kitchen
items.excerpt: A visible, well-planned recycling corner makes it easier for everyone at home to sort waste every day.
items.bodyMarkdown:
A good recycling corner does not need a lot of space. It mostly needs to be where waste actually appears.

## Start small
Two or three clearly identified containers are often enough to begin. There is no need to build a big system before you know what you will really use.

## Make the habit obvious
Place the bins close to the most active area of the kitchen: under the sink, beside the counter, or near the pantry.

## Reduce mistakes
- add a simple reminder to each bin
- keep sorting rules visible at eye level
- empty the bins before they overflow

## Keep it practical
Pick containers that are light, easy to clean, and easy to grab when it is time to empty them.

coverImage.alt: Recycling containers in a bright kitchen

items.title: Reduce single-use waste with an entryway routine
items.excerpt: A few well-placed items near the door can prevent many disposable purchases over the course of a week.
items.bodyMarkdown:
When the useful items are already waiting by the door, good decisions become much easier.

## Prepare the basics
Keep a reusable bag, a water bottle, and a lightweight container near the front door.

## Use repetition to your advantage
The idea is to make the better choice automatic, not heroic. A visible cue often works better than relying on memory alone.

## Adapt it to your real habits
If you often forget, hang the bag on the handle, place the bottle in front of your keys, or keep a container in your everyday bag.

## Stay realistic
Choose one or two items you can carry every day. A simple routine lasts longer than a perfect list.

items.title: Give jars and containers a second life
items.excerpt: Before recycling a glass jar or sturdy container, check whether it can still be useful at home.
items.bodyMarkdown:
Reusing a container you already have often avoids one more purchase while keeping the home organized.

## Reuse before recycling
The best container is often already in your cupboard. A sturdy jar may stay useful for much longer before it needs to be recycled.

## Simple uses that actually help
- dry food storage
- leftovers in the fridge
- screws or small hardware
- pencils, brushes, or craft supplies

## Keep only what proves useful
Save a few practical sizes and recycle the rest. The point is not to collect more objects, but to make better use of the ones you already own.

## Keep the system easy
Create one small zone for reusable containers so you always know what is available.

items.title: Prepare better for collection day
items.excerpt: A quick check the night before helps prevent missed items and makes collection morning smoother.
items.bodyMarkdown:
Collection day usually feels easier when the decisions are already made the evening before. A short routine is enough to create that calm.

## Check the night before
Take a few minutes to bring out what is ready and reorganize what still needs sorting.

## Group the right streams
Make sure each material is placed in the correct container before collection.

## A practical mini-checklist
- check the bins near the exit
- close any open bags or containers
- put aside anything that is not ready yet

## Keep the area clear
A dedicated spot near the exit makes it easy to see what needs to go out.

## Build a light routine
A simple habit the evening before reduces stress and mistakes in the morning.

items.title: Cool the home with shade and curtains
items.excerpt: A few simple habits around shade and airflow can reduce the need for air conditioning on hot days.
items.bodyMarkdown:
Reducing heat before it enters the home is often more effective than trying to remove it later.

## Close things at the right time
Shut curtains or blinds on the sunniest windows before rooms start to overheat.

## Use the cooler hours
Open windows early in the morning or later in the evening when outdoor air feels fresher.

## A simple trio to remember
- shade
- airflow
- fewer indoor heat sources

## Aim for comfort, not perfection
Even small changes on a few windows can noticeably improve comfort during warmer weeks.

coverImage.alt: Bright living room with curtains and large windows
